response.justification: The candidate brings impressive breadth as a polyglot senior developer with over 15 years of experience and genuine open-source engagement, but falls critically short on the two most decisive requirements for this role. German is listed as 'Basic,' well below the mandatory C1 minimum for a client-facing leadership position at a German IT consultancy. More structurally, every role on the resume is an individual contributor position — there is no demonstrated team leadership, headcount management, or P&L/project ownership that a Head of Development role requires. The absence of Python, UML, and formal architecture or requirements management experience further widens the gap.
strengths: 15+ years of polyglot development experience across web, mobile, desktop, and embedded systems demonstrates rare technical adaptability
Sustained open-source commitment (GISCollective, Fluent-Asserts, Trial, Vibe.dav) and Linux proficiency directly align with the company's stated focus on open-source and digital sovereignty
Active knowledge-sharing via workshops, FOSS4G talk, and in-office code kata sessions shows communication ability and a mentorship mindset
concerns: German proficiency is self-rated 'Basic,' a hard disqualifier against the stated C1 minimum required for client advisory and executive collaboration
No leadership or people-management experience anywhere in the employment history; all roles are senior individual contributor, not tech lead or engineering manager
Python, UML, and formal IT architecture or project management are absent from both the skills section and job descriptions, missing several of the role's core desired qualifications

key_factors.factor: No leadership or team management track record
